tax: Magma Roiler is placed at species rank beneath Roiler (Magmatic order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a molten-basalt body, a dark cooling crust over a glowing core, and stone-hard dormancy when cooled. It is separated from its sibling the Obsidian Slagling by its hot flowing molten body versus the Slagling's cooled, sharp-edged glass body; the two share the genus body-plan but never the cooled glass form. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: molten basalt held above its melting point by thermal-vent heat.
ap: Magma Roiler stands 2–4 m flowing lobe, several tonnes. Its silhouette is a roiling lava lobe with a crusting skin that cracks to show the glow. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a dark crust webbed with glowing orange cracks; radiant heat felt at several paces, scorching the air; and a sulphurous volcanic reek and the hiss of contact with damp. It moves with a slow viscous flow downhill, faster when freshly fed by a vent, and shows a deep orange core-glow through its cracking crust. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a cooled basalt lobe and a scorched, glazed track across the bench, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Magma Roiler persists in lava benches, thermal vents, and basalt flats of the warm lower gorge, where vent heat keeps it molten. It does not breed; it forms where fire-pressure and vent heat melt a basalt mass into a coherent flowing body, and recurs whenever the vent reheats the cooled basalt and it flows again. It sustains its mass on thermal-vent heat, drawing in rock melted into its mass, which it incorporates to grow. Range is bounded by the heated vent zone: outside that band the body cools, crusts over, and sets into dormant basalt. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a basalt bench that glows and flows uphill against the cooling gradient, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Magma Roiler is roused by a fresh pulse of vent heat or an intrusion onto its bench and otherwise lies a cooled basalt lobe that can re-melt without warning. It claims a single lava bench and answers intrusion with flowing to engulf and ignite, and radiating killing heat. Toward grey-folk it is slow but deadly by heat alone; survey crews report that Forgemasters read a bench's glowing cracks to judge whether the basalt is dormant or roused. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Magma Roiler: a body of molten basalt held above its melting point by thermal-vent heat; immune to fire and heat, and unharmed by piercing; and a defining weakness — rapid cooling — flooding or cold blast crusts and immobilises it. Its senses run through the medium itself: heat-gradient sensing; blind to cool, still objects. It tolerates open flame, sulphur, and darkness but fails at rapid cooling and full immersion. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the crusted lobe and glowing-crack core, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Magma Roiler are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it engulfs and incinerates with molten mass; it radiates killing heat at several paces; it melts and incorporates rock to repair and grow; and it crusts hard to shield itself, then re-melts. Each is a direct expression of vent-heated molten basalt, so it cannot, for instance, leap, fly, cross deep water, or persist once fully cooled and immersed. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Magma Roiler matters because it makes the warm lower benches lethal by radiant heat alone, and crews now read glowing cracks before crossing. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a heat-and-engulf hazard logged with lava benches, and its presence reshapes routes through the lower-gorge lava benches, now crossed only when the basalt reads dormant. Basalt that flows uphill against its cooling gradient is the Forgemasters' sign of a roused Roiler.
g: Magma Roiler (Lava fluens) is a molten-rock elemental that flows as a self-contained lava body and sets stone-hard when it cools. Documented from the lava benches and thermal vents of the warm lower gorge, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a glowing mass of molten basalt skinned in dark cooling crust — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Unlike open flame, it carries its own heat as molten rock and survives losing its fuel, merely cooling toward dormancy. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a cooled black basalt lobe frozen in mid-flow across the bench and no carcass.
